text:
… it goes to foreign nations. And I always try to come and speak a little to the people. I… Brother Baxter and them takes up the offering. And all the money in our campaigns has been a very strange thing. I… So happy to [unclear words] that I can say this here, but the Gospel of Jesus Christ, it hasn't been a meal ticket for me. If I thought that way about it, and that's the only alternative I had, I'd go back home, go back and get me a--my job back as a state game warden of Indiana, and go to work.
I preached twelve years back at Tabernacle at Jeffersonville without one penny's salary, offerings, or anything. I worked, and it was it. If it was somewhere now that I could get… Now, this seems very strange. But if I knew that while I was in Chicago, if I could get a job… Nobody'd hire me except maybe out here with a pick and shovel on the street. I'd do what I have to do to take care of myself. Now, that's not just to be humble; that's from my heart. See? And I could relax myself more. But, see, but then in these kind of meetings, you can't do that, you got to stay constantly with God, to know just what's going to be in the next meeting.
text_no:
… det går til fremmede nasjoner. Jeg prøver alltid å komme og tale litt til folkene. Bror Baxter og dem tar opp kollekten. Og alle pengene i våre kampanjer har vært en svært merkelig ting. Jeg er så glad for å kunne si dette her, men Jesu Kristi evangelium har ikke vært en middagsbillett for meg. Hvis jeg tenkte slik på det, og det var det eneste alternativet jeg hadde, ville jeg dra hjem, få tilbake jobben min som viltvokter i Indiana, og begynne å jobbe igjen.
Jeg forkynte i tolv år ved Tabernaklet i Jeffersonville uten en krone i lønn, kollekter, eller noe annet. Jeg jobbet, og det var det. Hvis det nå var et sted jeg kunne få… Dette virker veldig rart. Men hvis jeg visste at mens jeg var i Chicago, hvis jeg kunne få en jobb… Ingen ville ansette meg, bortsett fra kanskje som gatearbeider med spade. Jeg ville gjort det jeg måtte for å ta vare på meg selv. Det er ikke bare for å være ydmyk; det kommer fra hjertet. Og jeg kunne slappe av mer. Men i slike møter kan du ikke gjøre det, du må være konstant i bønn og i kontakt med Gud, for å vite nøyaktig hva som skal skje i neste møte.

text:
For remember me… In the larger services, where we set together, there's critics; there's everything. Satan's waiting for one false move, then of a… Everything that he can just pin right on that. See? So that's… I must be careful.
Just think, friends, in the meetings that passed, what if it wouldn't have been just exactly truth. Look what'd taken place.
Many of you has read my book back there called "Man Sent From God." You seen the testimonies and so forth. What would happened if some those close places where critics, maniacs, and everything run right to the platform and say, "Right this hour, I'll slay you. I'll take, mash every bone in your body." Well-able to do it… Ministers shrank and run from it. Stand there and see Almighty God strike their life, and that person fall to the floor [unclear words]
text_no:
Husk på meg... I de store møtene, der vi samles, finnes det kritikere; det finnes alt mulig. Satan venter på én feil, så kan han gripe fatt i det. Se? Så jeg må være forsiktig.
Tenk bare, venner, på møtene som har vært: Hva hvis det ikke hadde vært absolutt sant? Se hva som kunne ha skjedd.
Mange av dere har lest boken min, "Man Sent From God." Dere har sett vitnesbyrdene og så videre. Hva ville skjedd i de kritiske øyeblikkene når kritikere, galninger og andre stormet frem til plattformen og sa, "Akkurat nå vil jeg drepe deg. Jeg vil knuse hvert bein i kroppen din"? De var i stand til å gjøre det... Forkynnere trakk seg tilbake og løp fra det. Men så dere Den Allmektige Gud gripe inn og den personen falle til bakken.

text:
Stand and see them say, "It's psychology; you're reading the people's mind." Agents of different things, and most of the time, preachers run to the platform, say, "Now, that's psychology. You're only reading the people's mind." But you see power of Almighty God move come down and move that thing back (See?), where it…
Sweden, Finland, Africa, wherever you go, the opposition's always there: Satan. That's the reason many people… You don't know how I'd like to come and visit with many of you. I long to do that, but in this case… Now, I can't compare my life and tell you to live the way I do. See? I have to live the way He wants me to.
text_no:
Se hvordan de sier: "Det er psykologi; du leser folks tanker." Agenter av ulike ting, og for det meste, løper forkynnere til plattformen og sier: "Nå, det er psykologi. Du leser bare folks tanker." Men du ser Guds Allmektige kraft komme ned og flytte på det (se?), der det...
Sverige, Finland, Afrika, uansett hvor du drar, er motstanden alltid der: Satan. Det er grunnen til at mange mennesker... Dere aner ikke hvor lyst jeg har til å komme og besøke mange av dere. Jeg lengter etter å gjøre det, men i dette tilfellet... Nå, jeg kan ikke sammenligne mitt liv og be dere leve slik jeg gjør. Forstår dere? Jeg må leve slik Han vil at jeg skal leve.

text:
Now, I either can be the servant of man or the servant of God. In order to be the servant of God, I must abstain. No matter how much I love you, I must abstain from people. I'm not an isolationist. You don't know my heart, how I love people. But I have to stay away from people in order to stay with God. No matter who the person is, even my own people… My own brother drove up here the other day, my wife setting in with them. By the way my brother [unclear words] and my brother in flesh. Said, "You haven't been in my house for years." My mother cried and said. "Billy, why don't you visit them?" But I… That's my mother, but I love her. But the reasons…
There's a lady setting in this [unclear words] this afternoon, which is my wife. I promised God and her that all my affective love would go to her, for that part as a woman. I promised that.
Now, if I go down the street and a young lady comes along and says, "Are you, Brother Branham?" "Yes, sir." "Why, I love you." Well, the first place, if I thought I loved--had love for the girl it would be false. My first duty is the love that I pledged to my wife. I must think of that first. That's first. Then I… No matter how much I think here, I know it can't be right, because this has to be right first. If it's contrary to that, I must take this first. That's what I promised God. And that's the way it is with truth, and the Gospel, and Christ, and people. I love people with all my heart, but my first love is to Christ. My first duty is to Him. Therefore, I must serve Him. You understand, don't you?
text_no:
Nå, jeg kan enten være tjener for mennesker eller tjener for Gud. For å være Guds tjener, må jeg avstå fra visse ting. Uansett hvor mye jeg elsker dere, må jeg avstå fra mennesker. Jeg er ikke en tilbaketrukket person. Dere kjenner ikke mitt hjerte, hvor mye jeg elsker mennesker. Men jeg må holde meg unna mennesker for å være nær Gud. Uansett hvem personen er, selv mine egne slektninger… Min egen bror kjørte hit en dag, og min kone satt sammen med dem. Forresten, min bror og min bror i kjøttet. Han sa, "Du har ikke vært i mitt hus på flere år." Min mor gråt og sa, "Billy, hvorfor besøker du dem ikke?" Men jeg… Det er min mor, og jeg elsker henne. Men grunnene…
En dame som sitter her i ettermiddag, er min kone. Jeg lovet Gud og henne at all min kjærlighet som kvinne skulle gå til henne. Jeg lovet det.
Nå, om jeg går ned gaten og en ung dame kommer bort og sier, "Er du, Bror Branham?" "Ja." "Jeg elsker deg." Vel, om jeg tenkte at jeg elsket--hadde kjærlighet for jenta, ville det være falskt. Min første plikt er kjærligheten jeg lovet min kone. Jeg må tenke på det først. Det er det viktigste. Uansett hva jeg tenker her, vet jeg at det ikke kan være rett, fordi dette må være rett først. Hvis det er i strid med dette, må jeg velge dette først. Det er hva jeg lovet Gud. Og slik er det med sannhet, Evangeliet, Kristus og mennesker. Jeg elsker mennesker med hele mitt hjerte, men min første kjærlighet er til Kristus. Min første plikt er til Ham. Derfor må jeg tjene Ham. Dere forstår, ikke sant?

text:
And these missionary offerings, I've tried to spend the money… Never took a offering in my life, never took a offering in my life.
I remember one time. I tell this sometimes. My wife gets after me, so she just might as well ready to get after me again today, because here it is.
Right after we'd been married about two years. You know, the depression times was hard. I… We just couldn't make ends meet. Do you ever get that place? We all have. I believe you have. And I was a getting about, oh, about sixteen, eighteen dollars a week, and children to take care of and everything, I thought, "My, what will I do?" So I said, "You know what? I'm going to take an offering tonight."
She said, "Well, I'm going over to watch you do that." I'd never taken one, but she said…
text_no:
Jeg har forsøkt å bruke misjonsoffer til gode formål. Aldri har jeg tatt opp et offer i hele mitt liv.
En gang husker jeg spesielt godt. Noen ganger forteller jeg denne historien, og min kone blir alltid litt irritert av den, så hun må nok regne med å bli irritert igjen i dag.
Dette skjedde omtrent to år etter at vi hadde giftet oss. Tider var tøffe på grunn av depresjonen. Vi klarte knapt å få endene til å møtes. Har du noen gang vært i den situasjonen? Jeg tror vi alle har vært der. Jeg tjente omtrent seksten til atten dollar i uken, med barn å forsørge og alt annet, og jeg tenkte: "Hva skal jeg gjøre?"
Så sa jeg: "Vet du hva? I kveld skal jeg ta opp et offer."
Hun svarte: "Vel, jeg skal se på at du gjør det." Jeg hadde aldri gjort det før, men hun ville se...

text:
When we built the little church, right in the time, 1932, and I borrowed the money to put it up, and I never took an offering one time. A little box on the back of the church said, "Insomuch As Ye Have Done Unto To The Least Of These My Little Ones, You've Done It To Me." I never told the people that was there, but when my payments come due it was there. That's the life of faith. You trust God for it. See, just what I did.
And then, I remember that night I went over, and I was going to take an offering. Now, not cause the people wouldn't give to me. Sure, they'd die for me. But they… If it was necessary, they would--they would do that. But I didn't want it. I was young and worked, had a job. Rest them was working, why not I? So didn't interfere with me much in my ministry, so I went ahead.
But I remember that night, I said to the… "Well, friends," I said, "I kind of hate to say this, but I--I kind of hit a snag. I--I--I--I… We'll take up an offering." I said, "Brother Wisehart, would you get my hat?" They didn't have any collection plates. "Will you take my hat?"
Said, "Yes, sir, Billy." And he looked up at me kind of strange, went over and got my hat. I looked down, there's a little lady by the name of Ware setting right close [unclear words] A little old mother had [unclear words] little checkered aprons on, the pocket on the inside of the aprons (You know?), here on the side. You know what I'm talking about, some of you mothers. So I seen her reach down pull this little apron back and get out one of these little pocketbooks that snaps on the top. You know? She undone this little pocketbook and begin to fumble around with them nickels in there. My, I couldn't take that. That would've haunted me the rest of my life, I took that poor old thing's money. I looked at her, and great big lumps swelled up in my throat. And I said, "Now, friends, I was just trying your faith. I didn't mean that." I said [unclear words] My wife setting there looking to see how I was getting along. You know?
text_no:
Da vi bygde den lille menigheten i 1932, lånte jeg pengene til å sette den opp, og jeg tok aldri en eneste kollekt. En liten boks bak i menigheten sa: "Insomuch As Ye Have Done Unto To The Least Of These My Little Ones, You've Done It To Me." Jeg fortalte aldri folkene at den var der, men når betalingene kom forfall, så var pengene der. Det er livet i tro. Du stoler på Gud for det. Se, akkurat hva jeg gjorde.
Jeg husker også kvelden jeg vurderte å ta opp en kollekt. Ikke fordi folkene ikke ville gi til meg—selvfølgelig ville de det. De ville dø for meg om nødvendig. Men jeg ønsket det ikke. Jeg var ung, hadde en jobb og alle de andre arbeidet, så hvorfor ikke jeg? Det forstyrret ikke tjenesten min mye, så jeg fortsatte.
Men jeg husker den kvelden da jeg sa: "Vel, venner," sa jeg, "jeg liker egentlig ikke å si dette, men jeg har truffet en hindring. Vi må ta opp en kollekt." Jeg sa, "Bror Wisehart, kan du hente hatten min?" Vi hadde ikke noen kollektplater. "Kan du hente hatten min?"
Han svarte, "Ja, Billy," og så litt merkelig på meg før han gikk for å hente hatten. Jeg så ned og der satt en liten dame ved navn Ware. En liten gammel mor med rutete forkle med en lomme på innsiden av forkleet hennes. Dere vet hva jeg snakker om, noen av dere mødre. Jeg så henne rekke ned, trekke forkleet til side og hente fram en liten lommebok som knipser på toppen. Hun åpnet den og begynte å lete etter mynter der inne. Å, jeg kunne ikke ta det. Det ville ha hjemsøkt meg resten av livet om jeg tok pengene fra denne stakkars gamle damen. Jeg så på henne, og en stor klump satte seg i halsen. Jeg sa, "Nå, venner, jeg testet bare troen deres. Jeg mente det ikke." Min kone satt der og så hvordan jeg klarte meg.

text:
There's an old man. He's usually at every one of these meetings around here. I don't know whether he's here or not. His name is John Ryan. Not the John Rhyn that he was blind. It's another John Ryan. You'd think he belonged to the House of David; he wears long hair and long beard. And he was at the Hammond meeting and the Zion meetings. He may be here somewhere for all I know.
He come down to see me. So he rode a bicycle down. Bicycle probably cost him three dollars. It was a--it was a real… You know? And here he come down with that long hair blowing, the beard on one side, coming down to see me. Well, I think it backslid while he was down there.
Now, that's a mouthful for a Baptist to say, isn't it? Backslid. But he… The bicycle did anyhow. And he stood up and said, "Brother Bill, you can just have that thing." And he hitchhiked back.
So he give me the bicycle. And after I went home that night, the wife said, "I thought you was going to take up that offering?"
Now, Monday, I thought, "What am I going to do now?" I went and got me a can of paint for about fifteen cents, and I doctored up the old bicycle and painted it, and sold it for ten dollars. Didn't have to have the offering after all. So… Now, that…
But just feelings that I couldn't take… I knowed that poor little old woman was going to drop in them pennies and things, maybe she's been saving that for something else. And me a young man and her sixty-five years old, or something, I couldn't do that.
So we try to do the same way. Our services is not to bum people for money. If ever one time I ever hear of one of my managers begging for money, that's the end of the manager. We don't have no more managers like that. Because when God don't supply my needs out here, He wants me to go home. And so we--and wait for another vision.
text_no:
Det er en eldre mann som vanligvis er tilstede på alle disse møtene. Jeg vet ikke om han er her i kveld. Hans navn er John Ryan. Ikke den blinde John Rhyn, men en annen John Ryan. Du skulle tro han tilhørte Davids hus, med sitt lange hår og skjegg. Han deltok både på møtet i Hammond og møtene i Zion. Kanskje han er her et sted, for alt jeg vet.
Han kom ned for å møte meg, syklet hele veien, trolig med en sykkel som ikke kostet mer enn tre dollar. Og her kom han, med det lange håret blafrende og skjegget på den ene siden, for å besøke meg. Vel, jeg tror sykkelen fikk problemer mens han var der nede.
Det er ganske bemerkelsesverdig for en baptist å si, ikke sant? Men det var sykkelen som hadde problemer, ikke ham. Han reiste seg og sa: "Bror Bill, du kan bare ha den sykkelen." Så haiket han hjem igjen.
Han ga meg sykkelen. Da jeg kom hjem den kvelden, sa kona: "Jeg trodde du skulle ta opp den kollekten?"
Mandag tenkte jeg: "Hva skal jeg gjøre nå?" Jeg kjøpte en boks med maling for cirka femten cent, pusset opp den gamle sykkelen og solgte den for ti dollar. Jeg trengte ikke kollekten likevel.
Men jeg følte at jeg ikke kunne ta imot pengene... Jeg visste at de fattige, eldre damene ville legge penger i kollekten som kanskje var spart til noe annet. Og jeg som en ung mann kunne ikke få meg til å ta pengene deres.
Våre møter er ikke for å tigge penger fra folk. Hvis jeg noen gang hører at en av mine ledere ber om penger, er det slutten for den lederen. Vi har ikke ledere som gjør slikt. Fordi når Gud ikke lenger gir meg det jeg trenger, vil Han at jeg skal dra hjem og vente på en ny visjon.

text:
So, I'm not very good at that Brother Boze, I'm… I see I'm probably too close to it. I remember the first one of those that I ever tried to talk through, I was scared to death. I never seen nothing like that. I was at a Pentecostal meeting, and they had just invited me to the platform. I didn't know how close, and I was scared of the thing. And I was looking at it, and getting back, and it was over here around Mishawaka. [Brother Boze speaks.] Yes. All right.
Now, [Brother Branham speaks aside to someone.] Yes, sir. It probably is, because I sure don't know nothing about it. I'm sure of that. All right.
Say, you know, money plays a big part, after all, doesn't it, brother? I was sure to get it done now. That's fine. Thank you, brother. That's very nice.
text_no:
Så, jeg er ikke særlig god på det, Bror Boze. Jeg ser at jeg sannsynligvis er for nær det. Jeg husker den første gangen jeg prøvde å snakke om det, var jeg livredd. Jeg hadde aldri sett noe lignende. Jeg var på et pinsemøte, og de hadde nettopp invitert meg til plattformen. Jeg visste ikke hvor nært, og jeg var redd for hele greia. Jeg så på det og trakk meg tilbake. Det var her borte rundt Mishawaka. 
Ja, all right.
Nå, [Bror Branham snakker til noen.] Ja, sir. Det er nok tilfelle, for jeg vet helt sikkert ingenting om det. Jeg er sikker på det. All right.
Si, vet du, penger spiller en stor rolle, gjør de ikke, bror? Jeg sørget for å få det gjort nå. Det er fint. Takk, bror. Det var veldig snilt.

text:
Now, this afternoon while we're--are here… Brother Baxter said, "Well now, Brother Branham, for a missionary…" Now, I couldn't… I could [unclear words] If it had to come time for me to take up an offering for the Lord, I could do it. But I couldn't do it for myself.
But here not long ago, there was a great sum of money (Many of you heard about it.) was given to me, and I refused to look at it. I could have made all of you happy, just pick through [unclear words] now among us… A million five hundred thousand dollars, see. I even refused to look at it. See? They packed an article of it in the paper. But I didn't want it. It isn't money. A man that gets his mind on money, popularity, and things like that, he's surely on the outside.
There's three things I've noticed in reading history, that wrecks a minister's life from the start: one of them is money, the love of it. The next is popularity. When he thinks he's something, when he's nothing anyhow. And the next is women. That's right.
Man takes money, women, popularity. That's been the three major things that God's had trouble with His children. Many of the other things that goes along with it too. But the main thing is when God begins to bless a brother and give him something a little more than--or something another to help the people, then the man begins to think, "See who I am?" Right then he's on his road down.
You want me to tell you how to get up? Get down. The way up is go down. He that humbles himself, God will exalt. Is that right? He that exalts himself will be made abased.
text_no:
I ettermiddag mens vi er her... Bror Baxter sa: "Nå, Bror Branham, for en misjonær..." Nå kunne jeg... jeg kunne [uklare ord] Hvis det måtte bli tid for meg å ta opp en offergave for Herren, kunne jeg gjøre det. Men jeg kunne ikke gjøre det for meg selv.
Men her for ikke lenge siden ble en stor sum penger (mange av dere har hørt om det) gitt til meg, og jeg nektet å se på den. Jeg kunne ha gjort dere alle glade ved bare å plukke ut [uklare ord] nå blant oss... En million fem hundre tusen dollar, ser du. Jeg nektet til og med å se på det. De trykte en artikkel om det i avisen, men jeg ville ikke ha det. Det er ikke pengene det handler om. En mann som får tankene sine på penger, popularitet og slike ting, er definitivt på utsiden.
Det er tre ting jeg har lagt merke til ved å lese historie som ødelegger en tjeners liv fra starten: en av dem er penger, kjærligheten til dem. Den neste er popularitet. Når han tror han er noe, men egentlig ikke er noe. Og den neste er kvinner. Det er riktig. Mann tar penger, kvinner, popularitet. Det har vært de tre hovedtingene Gud har hatt problemer med blant Sine barn. Mange andre ting følger med det også. Men hovedproblemet er når Gud begynner å velsigne en bror og gir ham noe litt mer enn - eller noe annet for å hjelpe folket, da begynner mannen å tro, "Se hvem jeg er?" Akkurat da er han på vei ned.
Vil du at jeg skal fortelle deg hvordan du kommer opp? Gå ned. Veien opp er å gå ned. Den som ydmyker seg, Gud vil opphøye. Er det ikke riktig? Den som opphøyer seg selv, vil bli ydmyket.

text:
Now, to speak in the afternoon like this… I am not a preacher. When I come behind Brother Baxter, and Starr, and Brother Boze, these other ministers setting here, who are men who are educated, and my little grammar school education don't go very far; I tell you.
At Fort Wayne meeting, I never will forget it. I was out there just a preaching away (You know?), and I come back behind the curtains after the service was go to rest up; I was too weak to walk back. And there was a fine scholar walked up to me, said, "Brother Branham", said, "your grammar's terrible."
I said, "Yes, sir. I know that." I said, "I just had a seventh grade education. My father had died and I had to take care of ten…"
"Oh, that's no excuse." He said, "My," said, "the mistakes that you made tonight." Said, "You should--you should know better grammar than that. The crowd that you're speaking for," said, "why, it's a disgrace."
I guess he was part of Mr. Webster you know, so I said, "Well, sir," I said, "I know that's right." I said, "But…"
He said, "Well, you could take a correspondence course."
I said, "Yes, sir. But you see other times," I said, "I was… I had to take care of a big family and everything, and now to make ends meet," and I said, "And now," I said, "the Lord's work is so--keeps me so busy," I said, "I don't have time to take it."
He said, "Oh, that's no excuse." He said, "I heard you tonight out there saying, 'All to you people that come by this polepit tonight, believing will get healed.'"
I said, "Yes, sir. What was wrong with that?" I don't know the difference.
And he said, "Why, you should not say, polepit"; said, "you should say pulpit." He said, "Those people would appreciate you more if you said 'pulpit.'"
But he pinched me a little too hard. You know? So I put my hand over on his shoulder; I said, "Brother, I don't mean to hurt your feelings (See?), but them people out there don't care whether I say, polepit or pulpit. They want me to preach the Gospel and produce what I'm talking about. They see Jesus Christ; that's all they care about." That's right [unclear words]. Well now, I… That's the reason I don't say I'm a minister or a preacher.
text_no:
Å tale her på ettermiddagen... Jeg er ikke en forkynner. Når jeg kommer etter Bror Baxter, Starr og Bror Boze, og disse andre utdannede predikantene som sitter her, føler jeg at min beskjedne folkeskoleutdanning ikke rekker langt; det må jeg innrømme.
På Fort Wayne-stevnet vil jeg aldri glemme en episode. Jeg var der ute og forkynte (du vet?), og etter møtet gikk jeg bak scenen for å hvile; jeg var for svak til å gå tilbake. En lærd mann kom opp til meg og sa: "Bror Branham, grammatikken din er fryktelig."
Jeg svarte: "Ja, det vet jeg. Jeg har bare fullført syvende klasse. Min far døde, og jeg måtte ta vare på en stor familie."
"Å, det er ingen unnskyldning," sa han. "De feilene du gjorde i kveld," fortsatte han, "du bør vite bedre grammatikk enn det. Publikumet du taler til, det er en skam."
Jeg antar at han var slekt med Mr. Webster, så jeg sa: "Ja, det er sant. Men..."
Han avbrøt: "Du kunne ta et korrespondansekurs."
Jeg svarte: "Ja, men jeg måtte ta vare på en stor familie for å få endene til å møtes, og nå er Herrens arbeid så krevende at jeg ikke har tid til det."
Han sa: "Ingen unnskyldning. Jeg hørte deg i kveld si: 'Alle dere som kommer forbi denne polepiten i kveld, troende vil bli helbredet.'"
Jeg spurte: "Hva var galt med det?" Jeg forstod ikke forskjellen.
Han rettet meg: "Du bør ikke si 'polepit'. Du bør si 'pulpit'. Folk ville sette mer pris på det hvis du sa 'pulpit'."
Men han klemte for hardt. Så jeg la hånden min på skulderen hans og sa: "Bror, jeg mener ikke å såre dine følelser, men de menneskene der ute bryr seg ikke om jeg sier 'polepit' eller 'pulpit'. De vil at jeg skal forkynne Evangeliet og vise hva jeg snakker om. De ønsker å se Jesus Kristus; det er alt de bryr seg om." Og derfor sier jeg ikke at jeg er en minister eller en forkynner.

text:
When I was a little boy, my father was a rider, and he used to follow rodeos and so forth, and ride fancy riding. He was a good shot with pistols, and I used to see him take out a row of cans, just take those little bitty cans, like that, and throw them out, like that, and take two guns and just keep rolling that can. Why, I couldn't hit a lard can like that with a rifle, so it… See what a difference it was. And a rider, why, he could just… I've seen him ride till the blood would fly out of his nose and ears. And I thought, "Well, my, that's… He…"
When I got to be a good sized boy, I said, "I'm going to be like my dad." So, we had an old plow horse there. I just lived in Indiana most of life. We had an old plow horse there; it was about fourteen years old. And plow with the old thing all day and we had an old watering trough cut out of a log. Did you ever see one of them, when you cut the old log? Well, look at the country folks here, would you? My, that's fine. Well, I'll take off my coat and my tie now.
text_no:
Da jeg var en liten gutt, var min far en rytter og deltok ofte i rodeoer og liknende aktiviteter. Han var en dyktig skytter med pistoler, og jeg pleide å se ham sette opp en rekke blikkbokser, kaste dem opp i luften og treffe dem mens de rullet med to pistoler. Jeg kunne ikke treffe en stor blikkboks med en rifle. Se hvilken forskjell det var! Som rytter kunne han holde på til blodet fløy fra nese og ører. Jeg tenkte, "For en imponerende mann han er."
Da jeg ble litt eldre, bestemte jeg meg for å bli som ham. Vi hadde en gammel ploghest hjemme. Jeg har bodd i Indiana mesteparten av livet. Denne gamle ploghesten var rundt fjorten år gammel. Jeg pleide å pløye hele dagen med den, og vi hadde en gammel vanntrau skåret ut av en trestamme. Har du noen gang sett en slik? Vel, se på alle de folkene fra landet her. Det er flott. Nå skal jeg ta av meg jakken og slipset.

text:
So that--our old watering trough, down where they have to pump the water. You know? One of the kids would have to go to pumping a half hour almost before the time was up to get enough water to water the horse.
So we'd get behind the barn, so dad couldn't see us. And I'd slip in and get his saddle (You know?) and throw it across the old horse after we watered it, pull the harness off, the gear. And then I'd reach, get me a handful of cockleburs, and stick them under the saddle (You know?), and pull down the girth. Climb up in the middle of this old horse with this straw hat. You know? And the poor old horse, so old and stiff and tired, he couldn't get his feet of the ground. He'd just bawl. And when he'd stand there and bawl. And I'd take my hat in my hand, and I'd holler and scream, and my brothers all setting around on fence posts, all of them setting there. I was a cowboy. My, I really could ride. That poor old horse, why, he was like riding a rocking horse out here, wouldn't be as bad.
text_no:
Vår gamle vanntrough, der vi måtte pumpe vann. Du vet? En av ungene måtte begynne å pumpe en halv time før tiden var ute for å få nok vann til hesten.
Så vi gjemte oss bak låven, slik at far ikke kunne se oss. Jeg snek meg inn og hentet salen hans (Du vet?) og kastet den over den gamle hesten etter at vi hadde vannet den, tok av selen. Så tok jeg en håndfull stikkelsbær og plasserte dem under salen (Du vet?), og strammet gjorden. Jeg klatret opp på den gamle hesten med stråhatten min. Du vet? Den stakkars gamle hesten, så gammel og sliten, klarte ikke å løfte føttene fra bakken. Han bare brølte.
Mens han sto der og brølte, tok jeg hatten i hånden, ropte og skrek, mens brødrene mine satt rundt på gjerdestolpene og så på. Jeg var en cowboy. Åh, jeg kunne virkelig ri. Den stakkars gamle hesten, det var som å ri på en gyngehest, det ville ikke vært så ille.

text:
So when I got to be about eighteen, I thought, "Well, I'm old enough now. I'm going out west to be a cowboy." Probably seen too many movies. And so I'd went out west. And I landed in Arizona just at the time of a rodeo. Well, I went down; I had a little money, and I'd go buy a pair of chaps. So they had to find a nice big pair. You know? And I put it on me like that. Just a lad anyhow (You know?), there was about eighteen inches of leather hanging out on the ground, when I got. Looked like one of these little bantam chickens (You know?) with them feathers down behind me, like that. I thought, "Well, they're too big here in Arizona for us Kentuckians."
So I got me a pair of Levis and went out, climbed up around the corral fence, where they was riding the horses at the rodeo. And I heard them say, "Now, the next is going to be the Kansas Outlaw. He's going be rode by So-and-so, and carry on like that, the caller went by. I looked along side of that fence there at all of these disfigured cowboys (You know?), that setting up there, weather-beaten. You know? Looked all along them, I thought, "That's where I belong up there with them guys." So I climbs down and gets up there and sets on this corral fence (You know?) with just Levis on, a hat, both ears hanging down, too big for me. So only cost about a dollar ninety-five cents, so it wasn't very much. You know? Well, I thought I was a real cowman, because I'd rode that old plow horse (You know?), and I thought, "I can do that too." I'd never seen a real outlaw rode. You know?
text_no:
Da jeg ble rundt atten år, tenkte jeg: "Nå er jeg gammel nok. Jeg drar vestover for å bli cowboy." Sannsynligvis hadde jeg sett for mange filmer. Så jeg dro vestover og kom til Arizona akkurat i tide til et rodeo. Jeg hadde litt penger og kjøpte meg et par chaps. De måtte finne et stort og fint par. Jeg tok dem på meg, men det hang omtrent 45 cm med lær ned på bakken. Jeg så ut som en liten storhøns med fjærene hengende bak meg. Jeg tenkte: "De er for store her i Arizona for oss fra Kentucky."
Så jeg kjøpte meg et par Levis og dro ut. Jeg klatret opp på gjerdet rundt innhegningen der de red hester på rodeoen. Jeg hørte dem si: "Nå kommer Kansas Outlaw. Han skal rides av Så-og-så," og slik fortsatte det. Jeg så langs gjerdet på alle de herjede cowboyene som satt der, værbitte. Jeg tenkte: "Det er der jeg hører hjemme, sammen med de gutta." Jeg klatret ned, satte meg på gjerdet med bare Levis og en hatt som begge ørene stakk ut under, for den var for stor for meg. Den kostet bare rundt 1,95 dollar, så det var ikke mye. Jeg følte meg som en ekte cowboy, for jeg hadde ridd på den gamle ploghesten hjemme og trodde jeg kunne gjøre det samme. Jeg hadde aldri sett en ekte villhest bli ridd.

text:
So after a while they was running this horse right into the chute, like that, the bull pen. And when it come out, this fellow was standing there, catch-as-can as he dropped down in the saddle there, as they girded the saddle on. And I heard that horse screaming and bawling and spitting, I thought, "Oh, oh. That ain't that old plow horse that I had at home."
When he jumped out of there, this fellow jumped right in the saddle; he made about two side wheels, like that and done a sunfish; and saddle, man, and all went out. When he hit the ground the pick-ups got the horse and the ambulance got the man. The blood was running out of his nose and ears and everything. Here come the caller by, said, "I will give any man fifty dollars who will ride him thirty seconds."
Well, I… Come riding down through there, said, "All right." All them cowboys setting on the fence… Said, "I will give any man fifty dollars who will ride him thirty seconds." That would be like offering two thousand today nearly. Boy, that fifty dollars sounded awful good. He looked right at me and said, "Are you a rider?"
I said, "No, sir." That wasn't no plow horse.
text_no:
Etter en stund løpte de denne hesten rett inn i innhegningen. Når den kom ut, sto en mann der og hoppet ned i salen når de hadde spent den fast. Jeg hørte hesten skrike og brøle, og jeg tenkte: "Å nei, dette er ikke den gamle plog-hesten jeg har hjemme."
Da hesten spratt ut, hoppet mannen rett i salen. Hesten gjorde et par siderullinger og en baklengs salto. Salen, mannen og alt gikk i bakken. Når han traff jorden, kom pick-upene for å hente hesten og ambulansen for å hente mannen. Blodet rant fra nese og ører og overalt. En opproper kom forbi og sa: "Jeg gir femti dollar til den som rir ham i tretti sekunder."
Vel, jeg kom ridende nedover der, og sa: "All right." Alle cowboyene satt på gjerdet… Han sa: "Jeg gir femti dollar til den som rir ham i tretti sekunder." Det ville nesten være som å tilby to tusen kroner i dag. De femti dollarene hørtes veldig fristende ut. Han så direkte på meg og spurte: "Er du en rytter?"
Jeg svarte: "Nei, herr." Dette var ingen plog-hest.

text:
That's the reason now when I talk about… When I'm home around the church, say, "You a preacher?"
"Yep."
But when I get out around these preachers, like this, say, "You a preacher?"
I say, "No, sir."
No. But I do love the Word of the Lord, and I love to worship Him anywhere when we have fellowship around the Word of the Lord. Now, a few little texts that usually I use out like this. Home I go into the Bible, teaching the Old Testament and New, and so forth, not in [unclear words] Greek and Hebrew, because I don't know it. It's just the way I see it here, and the way the Holy Spirit will reveal to me.
text_no:
Når jeg er hjemme i menigheten og noen spør, "Er du en forkynner?", svarer jeg, "Ja." Men når jeg er blant andre forkynnere som her, og de spør, "Er du en forkynner?", svarer jeg, "Nei, sir."
Jeg elsker likevel Herrens Ord, og jeg elsker å tilbe Ham overalt hvor vi har fellesskap rundt Herrens Ord. Vanligvis bruker jeg noen få små tekster som dette. Hjemme går jeg inn i Bibelen, underviser i både Det gamle testamentet og Det nye testamentet, og så videre, ikke på gresk og hebraisk, for det kan jeg ikke. Jeg formidler det slik jeg forstår det her, og slik Den Hellige Ånd åpenbarer det for meg.

text:
This afternoon, if the Lord's willing, I'd like to take a little text here. Maybe I--some of you here might've heard me preach on it. I've preach on four or five different little subjects around over the country, something like there of "Come and See a Man." I usually use that sometime. That's… "The Woman At The Well," and usually try to approach it in a different manner, so that each time it'll be different. A little text like that, because I don't want to get too far off on that side, because when I go back tonight to take back the services, it's a different anointing and it upsets me some times. See?
So or "Believest Thou This? or "Four Ways Of Seeing God," or "Then He Cried," or little things like that.
So this afternoon I want to read out of Saint John the--the--of the… "Even Now, Lord."
text_no:
Denne ettermiddagen, hvis Herren vil, vil jeg gjerne ta opp en liten tekst her. Kanskje noen av dere har hørt meg preke over dette tidligere. Jeg har præket om fire eller fem forskjellige små emner rundt om i landet, noe á la "Kom og Se en Mann." Jeg bruker ofte det tidvis. Det er som oftest "Kvinnen ved Brønnen," og jeg prøver vanligvis å nærme meg det på en annen måte hver gang, slik at det blir annerledes hver gang. En slik liten tekst, fordi jeg ikke vil gå for langt inn på den siden, ettersom når jeg kommer tilbake i kveld for å ta tilbake møtene, er det en annen salvelse, og det forvirrer meg noen ganger.
Så, eller "Tror du Dette?" eller "Fire Måter å Se Gud på," eller "Så Ropte Han," eller små ting som det. Så denne ettermiddagen vil jeg lese fra Johannes-evangeliet, om ... "Selv Nå, Herre."

text:
I want to read out of Saint John the 11th chapter. And the reading of the Word, God will produce the results by the reading of the Word. Don't you believe that? I have spoke on this somewhere before, maybe, in the neighborhood somewhere here, or somewhere across the country. If I have, I'm going to try to approach it in a different way. Let's take those…
And now, Brother Baxter's the one does the preaching. I just come down. It kind of relaxes me a little this afternoon and then for tonight.
text_no:
Jeg ønsker å lese fra Johannes 11. kapittel. Når Ordet leses, vil Gud skape resultater gjennom Sin Ord. Tror du ikke det? Jeg har kanskje snakket om dette før, muligens et sted i nærheten her eller et annet sted i landet. Hvis jeg har det, vil jeg prøve å tilnærme meg temaet på en annen måte denne gangen. La oss ta de...
Nå er det Bror Baxter som står for forkynnelsen. Jeg kommer bare ned for å slappe av litt denne ettermiddagen og dermed forberede meg til kvelden.

text:
The 11th chapter of Saint John, and let's begin about the 18th or 19th--18th verse, let's say, and read just a little portion there of the Scripture.
Now Bethany was nigh unto Jerusalem, about fifteen furlongs off:
And many of the Jews came to Martha and Mary, to comfort them concerning their brother.
Then Martha, as soon as she heard that Jesus was coming, went and met him: but Mary sat still in the house.
Then said Martha unto Jesus, Lord, if thou had been here, my brother had not died.
But I know, that even now, whatsoever thou will ask … God, God will give it thee.
Jesus said unto her, Thy brother shall rise again.
Martha said unto him, I know that he shall rise again in the resurrection at the last day.
Jesus said unto her, I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live:
… whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die. Believest thou this?
She said … Yea, Lord: I believe that thou art the Christ, the Son of God, which should come into the world.
Now, let's bow our heads just a moment, speak to the Author of this Book.
text_no:
Det står skrevet i det 11. kapittel av evangeliet etter Johannes. La oss begynne ved vers 18 og lese et lite utdrag fra Skriften.
"Betania var nær Jerusalem, omtrent femten stadier unna.
Mange av jødene hadde kommet til Marta og Maria for å trøste dem i sorgen over deres bror.
Da Marta hørte at Jesus kom, gikk hun for å møte Ham; men Maria ble sittende hjemme.
Marta sa til Jesus: 'Herre, hadde Du vært her, ville min bror ikke vært død.
Men også nå vet jeg at hva Du ber Gud om, vil Gud gi Deg.'
Jesus sa til henne: 'Din bror skal oppstå igjen.'
Marta sier til Ham: 'Jeg vet at han skal oppstå i oppstandelsen på den siste dag.'
Jesus sa til henne: 'Jeg er oppstandelsen og livet. Den som tror på Meg, skal leve om han enn dør,
og hver den som lever og tror på Meg, skal aldri noensinne dø. Tror du dette?'
Hun sa til Ham: 'Ja, Herre, jeg tror at Du er Messias, Guds Sønn, Han som skal komme til verden.'"
La oss bøye våre hoder et øyeblikk og tale til Forfatteren av denne Boken.

text:
Lord Jesus, Master of Life, Giver of all good gifts by Thy grace, we know that we are saved, not because of our merit, but because the grace of God has been shed abroad in our hearts, by the Holy Spirit. At one time we were aliens, cut off from God, Gentiles, worshiping dumb idols. But in due season Christ died, the Innocent for the guilty, taking us to the Father. And now, we are sons and daughters of God. It does not yet appear what we shall be like, but we know we shall have a body like His, for we shall see Him as He is.
Lord, how we groan in these bodies, to be clothed upon with immortality, to be clothed upon with His Spirit, that someday…
text_no:
Herre Jesus, Livets Mester, Giver av alle gode gaver ved Din nåde, vi vet at vi er frelst, ikke på grunn av vår fortjeneste, men på grunn av Guds nåde som er utøst i våre hjerter ved Den Hellige Ånd. En gang var vi fremmede, avskåret fra Gud, hedninger som tilba stumme avguder. Men i sin tid døde Kristus, den Uskyldige for de skyldige, og førte oss til Faderen. Nå er vi Guds sønner og døtre. Det er ennå ikke åpenbart hva vi skal bli, men vi vet at vi skal få et legeme likt Hans, for vi skal se Ham som Han er.
Herre, hvor vi stønner i disse kroppene, lengtende etter å bli kledd i udødelighet, å bli kledd i Hans Ånd, slik at en dag…

text: We know this, that in the Old Testament the blood of bulls and goats could not take away sin. It only covered sin. And the living just waiting in paradise for the day that sin should be taken away. But when Jesus came, the Son of God, the Messiah, He took away sin. He divorced it, never covered it, He took it away. Then, now, since then, if this earthly tabernacle be dissolved we have one waiting in glory; we go straight to the presence of the Father, there to be clothed in immortality. Oh, if this earthly tabernacle, if it should be taken away, this afternoon… Born in sin, shaped in iniquity, come to the world speaking lies, sinners by nature, then God has redeemed us and redeemed our soul, and has a blessed body waiting for us, that'll never be sick, even above temptation, a body like His own glorious body, then we shall see Him as He is.
text_no: I Det Gamle Testamentet kunne blodet av okser og geiter ikke ta bort synd; det dekket kun over synden. De rettferdige levde i påvente i paradis på den dagen da synden skulle fjernes. Men da Jesus, Guds Sønn, Messias, kom, fjernet Han synden. Han skiltet den fra oss, dekket den aldri bare til; Han tok den bort. Siden da, dersom dette jordiske tabernaklet blir oppløst, har vi et som venter i herlighet. Vi går rett til Faderens nærvær, der vi skal bli kledd i udødelighet. Å, hvis dette jordiske tabernaklet skulle bli tatt bort i ettermiddag… Født i synd, formet i misgjerning, kom til verden talende løgner, syndere av natur. Men Gud har forløst oss, forløst vår sjel, og har et velsignet legeme som venter oss, som aldri vil bli sykt, utenfor fristelse, et legeme som Hans eget herlige legeme. Da skal vi se Ham som Han er.

text:
Now, Father, we're approaching Your Word. There's no man was able to open the book, or to loose the seals thereof. But the Lamb which was slain from the foundation of the world, He was worthy. And He came and took the book out of the right hand of Him, and opened the book and loosed the seals. And now, may the Holy Spirit come and get on these words, that we've just read. And may You speak, today, and go right out over this audience here, and may the Holy Spirit give to the church just what we have need of: faith.
Prepare a service for tonight, Lord. May tonight be the greatest service, not because of us here, but because that Thou art here. May it be the greatest service that this Philadelphian church has had since it's been established. May there be a shaking go forth tonight, that'll have the holy God of heaven to set us back there in a big stadium, an auditorium somewhere, that'll shake Chicago for three or four months. Grant it, Lord.
Do the exceedingly abundantly today. Prepare the hearts just now. Break up the ground, grub out all the greenbriers, and the stickers, and the thorns, and roots of bitterness, and bring down the Holy Spirit, so tonight, that the Angel of the God might sweep over this building in a great meeting, for we ask it in Jesus' name. Amen.
Thank you, brother.
text_no:
Nå, Far, nærmer vi oss Ditt Ord. Ingen mann var i stand til å åpne boken eller løse seglene i den. Men Lammet som ble slaktet fra verdens grunnvoll, Han var verdig. Han kom og tok boken fra den Høyeres høyre hånd og åpnet den, og løste seglene. Måtte Den Hellige Ånd nå komme og fylle disse ordene vi nettopp har lest. Måtte Du tale i dag, og gå ut over dette publikum her, og la Den Hellige Ånd gi menigheten akkurat det vi trenger: tro.
Forbered en samling for i kveld, Herre. Måtte kvelden være den største samlingen, ikke på grunn av oss, men fordi Du er her. Måtte det bli den største samlingen denne filadelfiske menigheten har hatt siden den ble etablert. Måtte det gå en rystelse i kveld, slik at den hellige Gud i himmelen setter oss tilbake der på et stort stadion, eller et auditorium, som vil ryste Chicago i tre eller fire måneder. Gi oss det, Herre.
Gjør det over all forventning i dag. Forbered hjertene akkurat nå. Bryt opp jorda, fjern alle tornebusker, stikkere og bitterhetsrøtter, og bring ned Den Hellige Ånd, slik at Guds Engel kan feie over dette bygget i en stor sammenkomst i kveld, for vi ber om dette i Jesu navn. Amen.
Takk, Bror.

text:
To the approach to the Word now just for a few moments… I'll take my watch off. I don't know where the clock is. It's a… And I'll try to stay no longer than I possibly can now. So that you'll have ample time to get ready.
And my son, just a few moments ago, asked me to announce to the people, that wants prayer cards for tonight, to be here at six o'clock tonight, if possible, to receive prayer cards; those who are afflicted and in need, and to be here at six o'clock tonight to receive the prayer cards.
Now, our Scripture goes back today just before the… Or this time was in the ministry of our Lord. He had just become very famous because of God being with Him. He was a Man, not no beauty we should desire Him. He was--He wasn't much to look upon, probably a little frail-looking Fellow. And the Bible said that He was not beautiful, in the way, a big six-footer like, or seven-footer like Saul. But He was a small Man.
text_no:
Nå tilnærmer vi oss Ordet for noen få øyeblikk... Jeg tar av meg klokken. Jeg vet ikke hvor klokken er, men jeg skal prøve å ikke vare lengre enn nødvendig, slik at dere har god tid til å gjøre dere klare.
Min sønn ba meg nettopp om å kunngjøre til de som ønsker bønnekort til i kveld, at de bør være her klokken seks i kveld for å motta bønnekort; de som er syke eller i nød bør være her klokken seks i kveld for å motta bønnekort.
Vår Skriftlesning i dag tar oss tilbake til en tid i vår Herres tjeneste. Han hadde nettopp blitt svært kjent fordi Gud var med Ham. Han var en Mann uten noe utseende som vi skulle begjære Ham. Han var ikke mye å se på, sannsynligvis en litt spinkel Person. Bibelen sier at Han ikke var vakker i den forstand, som en stor mann på seks footer eller syv footer som Saul. Men Han var en liten Mann.

text:
The Bible said, "We hid, as it were, our face from Him, yet we did esteem Him, smitten--smitten, stricken, afflicted of God." But the works that God was doing in Him, was outstanding, that the people knew that He come from God.
For even the great teacher said, that came to Him by night, Nicodemus by name. Said, "We know that Thou art a teacher come from God, because no man could do these things that You do, unless God was with him." They knowed beyond a shadow of doubt.
Now, the ministers of them days, said, now, "He's a psychiatrist. He's a mind-reader. He reads your mind. That's how He knows what's wrong with them people; He reads your mind." He did. And Jesus was a mind-reader. That shocked you, didn't it? But He was. He perceived their thoughts. Is that right? Well, what's mind-reading? See? Not one of these setting out here, one of these little women setting out on the corner, taking the palm of your hand; that's the substitute the devil's got, something to imitate it. That's the bogus dollar to the real dollar, see.
text_no:
Bibelen sier: "Vi skjulte, som det var, vårt ansikt for Ham, men likevel aktet vi Ham, slått--slått, straffet, plaget av Gud." Men de gjerningene Gud utførte gjennom Ham, var så bemerkelsesverdige at folket visste at Han kom fra Gud. 
Selv den store læreren, som kom til Ham om natten, Nikodemus var hans navn, sa: "Vi vet at Du er en lærer kommet fra Gud, for ingen kan gjøre disse gjerningene som Du gjør, med mindre Gud er med Ham." De visste det uten tvil.
Nå, prestene i de dager sa: "Han er en psykiater. Han er en tankeleser. Han leser andres tanker. Det er slik Han vet hva som feiler folk; Han leser deres tanker." Ja, det gjorde Han. Og Jesus var en tankeleser. Sjokkerte det deg? Men slik var det. Han gjennomskuet deres tanker. Er det ikke riktig? Hva er tankelesing, da? Det handler ikke om kvinner som sitter på hjørnet og leser i håndflaten din; det er djevelens erstatning, en imitasjon. Det er den falske pengeseddelen i forhold til en ekte seddel.

text:
Everything God has done, the devil's made a substitute for it, made something bogus, see. And that's the reason the two spirits in the last days, come right along, be formal and fundamental they miss the Spirit altogether (You see?), like that. And some of these on the radical side go plumb off into isms with it, but right in the middle of the road runs the true church of God, standing in and full of the power of God. There's where it's at.
Now, certainly, everything that God has, Satan has something to counterfeit it. See, he makes something off of it. And Jesus did perceive their thoughts, so therefore the great Orthodox of those days, said, "That Guy is the best fortuneteller in the country. He's Beelzebub, the prince of the devil." Is that right? Now, that's what the preachers said. And the great Bible teachers of that day said, "He is Beelzebub."
text_no:
Alt Gud har gjort, har djevelen laget en erstatning for, noe falskt, skjønner du. Derfor fører de to åndene i de siste dager til at man blir formell og grunnleggende og helt går glipp av Ånden. Noen på den radikale siden faller helt ut i sekterisme med det, men midt i veien går Guds sanne menighet, fylt av Guds kraft. Der er det.
Selvfølgelig har Satan en forfalskning for alt Gud har. Han lager noe falskt av det. Jesus oppfattet deres tanker, og derfor sa de store ortodokse på den tiden: "Den karen er den beste spåmannen i landet. Han er Beelzebub, djevelens fyrste." Er det riktig? Det var det predikantene sa. Og de store bibellærerne på den tiden sa: "Han er Beelzebub."

text:
Now, let's see what the devil said. The devil said, "He's the Son of God. We know who You are, the holy One of God." Is that right? And that case the devils had the more knowledge of God than the ministry did. Is that right?
When Paul and Silas was up there and they was doing some great works for God, there was a little old fortuneteller run out down the street, every day, and cried behind Paul. Said, "There's the men that tell us the way of life. That's men of God."
And here was the preachers, said, "They're impostors. They turn the world upside down. They're no good." And the… There was the ministry saying, "They're impostors." And the devil saying, "They're men of God." Which was right? See, the devil was right. Is that right? Sure he was. So you see, even them people…
You can get so much on the Word, you think, "Oh, the Word, the Word." That's right. But the Gospel came not in Word only; but through power and demonstration of the Holy Spirit, until the Word is made life, see.
Wheat's all right in your hand, but you never get a crop out of it. You got to bury it, and let it take on new life. Is that right?
text_no:
La oss se hva djevelen sa. Djevelen sa: "Han er Guds Sønn. Vi vet hvem Du er, den hellige fra Gud." Er det riktig? I det tilfellet hadde demonene mer kunnskap om Gud enn tjenesten gjorde. Er det riktig?
Da Paulus og Silas var der oppe og gjorde store gjerninger for Gud, løp en liten spåkvinne ut på gaten hver dag og ropte etter Paulus: "Der er mennene som viser oss veien til livet. Det er Guds menn."
Men forkynnerne sa: "De er bedragere. De snur verden opp ned. De er ikke gode." Her var tjenesten som sa: "De er bedragere." Og djevelen sa: "De er Guds menn." Hvem hadde rett? Ser du, djevelen hadde rett. Er det riktig? Jo, det er det.
Så ser du, selv disse menneskene... Du kan fokusere så mye på Ordet og tenke: "Å, Ordet, Ordet." Det stemmer. Men Evangeliet kom ikke bare i Ord, men gjennom kraft og demonstrasjon av Den Hellige Ånd, inntil Ordet blir liv.
Hvete er greit i hånden din, men du får aldri en avling ut av det. Du må begrave det og la det få nytt liv. Er det riktig?

text:
Now, the Word is right; this is God's way, of course. But just the Word, "The letter, killeth, but the Spirit giveth Life." See what I mean?
Now, and therefore, the enemy had to testify of God. In His ministry here He went forth, and things begin to happen. And then the churches all closed down on Him and said, "Now, if anybody goes over there to hear Him, we'll just get your… Hand your papers in. You'll have to go out. You're no more."
But there's some people by the name of Martha and Mary and Lazarus, brothers and sisters. And they heard Jesus, and they seen His miracles, so they didn't care whether He was--what the Orthodox said, what the church said, or nothing about it. They believed Him and they went with Him. And they believed Him, because they knew He was a Man of God. So He came to stay in their house, at Bethany.
text_no:
Nå, Ordet er riktig; dette er selvsagt Guds vei. Men bare Ordet, "Bokstaven slår i hjel, men Ånden gjør levende." Skjønner du hva jeg mener?
Derfor måtte fienden vitne om Gud. Under Hans tjeneste her, gikk Han fram, og ting begynte å skje. Så stengte menighetene dørene for Ham og sa, "Nå, hvis noen går dit for å høre Ham, leverer vi inn papirene dine. Du må dra ut. Du er ikke mer."
Men det finnes noen mennesker ved navn Marta, Maria og Lasarus, søsken. De hørte Jesus, og de så Hans mirakler. De brydde seg ikke om hva de ortodokse, hva menigheten, eller noen andre sa om det. De trodde på Ham og fulgte Ham. De trodde på Ham fordi de visste at Han var en Guds Mann. Så Han kom for å bo i deres hus i Betania.

text: And now, while He was living there with them, His ministry got so great, that one day He had to go away from the home. They crowded Him out, too much, too many people around the place. And the people would come to Him by the thousands, and He'd slip off away from them, go off somewhere else (See?), to be alone with the Father, to pray. The cities would call for Him, but He would go up in the mountain alone, see which way the Father would lead. He came to do the work of the Father. That's what we ought to be about, today, our Father's business (That's right.), not so much in the social ranks and so forth, but in the Father's business.
text_no: Mens Han bodde sammen med dem, ble Hans tjeneste så omfattende at Han en dag måtte forlate hjemmet. Det ble for mange mennesker rundt stedet, og de trengte Ham ut. Folk kom til Ham i tusentall, og Han trakk seg unna, dro et annet sted for å være alene med Faderen og be. Byene etterspurte Ham, men Han gikk opp i fjellet alene for å se hvilken vei Faderen ville lede Ham. Han kom for å gjøre Faderens arbeid. Det er det vi også burde være opptatte av i dag—vår Fars forretninger—ikke så mye de sosiale lagene og lignende, men i Faderens forretninger.

text:
Now, let's take this wonderful Jesus just a minute, and watch His coming. Just before He came, there was a… First, before His coming, there was announcement. The church had all got all back and all messed up, just about like it is today. And one having this, the Pharisees, Sadducees, Publicans, array, oh, all kinds of isms around. And that, even some man come up and claimed he was a great one, who led hundreds out in the wilderness, and they perished. You know the story. And all like that…
But it finally come the time for He to come, He… The remnant was waiting for Him. And one day… Let's watch.
I see an old man and a woman, way passed the age of bearing, the woman was. And she said, they had prayed for God all these years to take away her reproach, and she could bear children, as it was an honor for any Hebrew woman to have a child.
text_no:
La oss ta et øyeblikk og observere Jesu komme. Rett før Han kom, var det en kunngjøring. Menigheten hadde kommet på avveie og blitt kaotisk, omtrent som i dag. Fariseere, saddukeere, tollere og ulike sekter blomstret. Det var til og med en mann som hevdet å være stor, ledet hundrevis ut i ødemarken, hvor de omkom. Dere kjenner historien. Slike hendelser...
Men til slutt kom tiden for at Han skulle komme. Resten ventet på Ham. En dag... La oss se.
Jeg ser en gammel mann og en kvinne, den kvinnen var langt over barnealderen. De hadde bedt Gud i mange år om å ta bort hennes vanære slik at hun kunne få barn, da det var en ære for enhver hebraisk kvinne å få et barn.

text:
How they've changed that now. It's almost un--a dishonor to have a child. Now, isn't that right? You might not say amen, but it's the truth. A woman had rather pay a hundred dollars for a little old smashed-nosed dog, and give it a mother's love out here, than to fool with a baby. Now, you know that's the truth. Walk it down the street with a little sweater on it. And I seen one of the biggest clinics, I ever seen, nearly, out here, is a dog clinic. I don't figure it. I can't see it. All right. Anyhow, that's up between you and God.
Now, look. Then Zacharias was a righteous man, a priest. And he come to the house of God; in his office was in there to burn incense while the people were praying. And then, one day while he was burning incense, the time of the promise was drawing nigh. And God sent an Angel down to the altar. Do you believe in Angels? Yes, sir.
text_no:
Hvordan har dette forandret seg nå. Det er nesten en vanære å ha et barn. Er ikke det riktig? Du sier kanskje ikke amen, men det er sannheten. En kvinne betaler heller hundre dollar for en liten stygg hund og gir den en mors kjærlighet, enn å bry seg med en baby. Du vet at det er sant. Går med den nedover gaten med en liten genser på. Jeg har sett en av de største klinikkene jeg noen gang har sett, nesten som en hundeklinikk. Jeg forstår det ikke. Jeg kan ikke se det. Uansett, det er mellom deg og Gud.
Se nå. Så var Sakarias en rettferdig mann, en prest. Han kom til Guds hus; hans oppgave var å brenne røkelse mens folket ba. En dag, mens han brente røkelse, nærmet tiden for løftet seg. Gud sendte en Engel ned til alteret. Tror du på Engler? Ja, herr.

text:
Someone said, not long ago, said, "Brother Branham, you're mistaken about that Man that talks to you." Said, "That was the Lord.
I said, "It was an Angel." He said, "I am sent from the presence of God." He wasn't the Lord. He was an Angel.
"Oh," said, "that's all mistaken." Said, "Brother Branham, God in old times, in the Bible, spoke," said, "there was Daniel and all of them. Yeah, they were--had Angels and so forth, like that, but not New Testament doctrine." And this man was a man, said, "We speak where the Bible speaks, and silent where the Bible's silent."
I thought, "How silent He was on many things that the Bible spoke." I said, "Do you mean to tell me that you don't believe that, the Angels of God…?"
The man said, "No, sir. The Holy Spirit leads the church."
I said, "That's correctly. But they have ministering spirits sent from the presence of God."
He said, "Not in the New Testament, brother."
I said, "Oh, yes, in the New Testament."
He said, "The Holy Spirit, not Angels, the Holy Spirit…"
I said, "Look, I want to ask you something." I said, "It was the Angel of God who met Mary."
He said, "Yeah, but that was before Pentecost. After Pentecost," he said, "it was the Holy Spirit from then on."
text_no:
Noen sa, for ikke lenge siden, "Bror Branham, du tar feil om den Mannen som snakker til deg." Sa, "Det var Herren."
Jeg sa, "Det var en Engel." Han sa, "Jeg er sendt fra Guds nærvær." Han var ikke Herren. Han var en Engel.
"Oh," sa han, "det er helt feil." Sa, "Bror Branham, Gud talte i gamle tider, i Bibelen," sa, "det var Daniel og alle de andre." Ja, de hadde Engler og lignende, men ikke i Nytestamentlig doktrine." Og denne mannen sa, "Vi taler der Bibelen taler, og er tause der Bibelen er taus."
Jeg tenkte, "Hvor taus han var om mange ting Bibelen taler om." Jeg sa, "Mener du å fortelle meg at du ikke tror på Guds Engler?"
Mannen sa, "Nei, sir. Den Hellige Ånd leder menigheten."
Jeg sa, "Det er riktig. Men de har tjenende ånder sendt fra Guds nærvær."
Han sa, "Ikke i det Nye Testamentet, bror."
Jeg sa, "Å, jo, i det Nye Testamentet."
Han sa, "Den Hellige Ånd, ikke Engler, Den Hellige Ånd..."
Jeg sa, "Se, jeg vil spørre deg om noe." Jeg sa, "Det var Guds Engel som møtte Maria."
Han sa, "Ja, men det var før pinse. Etter pinse," sa han, "var det Den Hellige Ånd fra da av."

text:
I said, "Truly the Holy Spirit leads the church; that's truth. But look," I said, "do you believe that Philip had the baptism of the Holy Spirit?"
Do you believe it, all of you? Sure, he did. Well, when he was down in Samaria holding a revival, who was it that spoke to him and said, "Go out into the desert Gaza"? Was it the Holy Spirit? No, sir. An Angel of the Lord spoke to him. Is that right? "Go out to the desert Gaza, and speak."
Look, how many believes that Peter had the Holy Spirit? Let's see. You believe he had the baptism of the Holy Spirit? When he was in prison that night, and down at John Mark's house, was having a prayer meeting for him, who was it, like a light that shined in the window and opened the doors for him? Was it the Holy Spirit? The Angel of the Lord.
text_no:
Jeg sa: «Den Hellige Ånd leder virkelig menigheten; det er sant. Men se her,» fortsatte jeg, «tror du at Filip hadde dåpen i Den Hellige Ånd?»
Tror dere det, alle sammen? Selvsagt, det hadde han. Da han var i Samaria og holdt en vekkelse, hvem var det som talte til ham og sa: "Gå ut i ørkenen Gaza"? Var det Den Hellige Ånd? Nei, det var en Herrens Engel som talte til ham. Stemmer ikke det? "Gå ut i ørkenen Gaza, og tal."
Se her, hvor mange tror at Peter hadde Den Hellige Ånd? La meg se. Dere tror han hadde dåpen i Den Hellige Ånd? Da han satt i fengsel den natten, og de i Johannes Markus’ hus hadde et bønnemøte for ham, hvem var det som, som et lys, skinte inn gjennom vinduet og åpnet dørene for ham? Var det Den Hellige Ånd? Nei, det var Herrens Engel.

text:
How many believes Paul had the Holy Ghost? Well, when he was out there on the ship fourteen days and nights, no moon, stars, and all hope was gone, he went down in the galley to pray, he come back up. Said, "Have a good courage, for the Angel of God, Whose servant I am, stood by me last night, saying, 'Fear not, Paul.'" Is that right? The Angel of the Lord…
The whole book of Revelations was wrote by the witness of an Angel. "I, Jesus, have sent My Angel to testify of these things to write unto the church." Is that right?
John fell down to worship the Angel. The Angel said, "Do it not." Now, it's not Joseph Smith. But He said… A true Angel won't stand to be worshipped. You know that. No, sir. He said, "Worship God."
text_no:
Hvor mange tror at Paulus hadde Den Hellige Ånd? Vel, da han var ute på skipet i fjorten dager og netter, uten måne eller stjerner, da alt håp var ute, gikk han ned i byssa for å be. Han kom tilbake opp og sa: "Vær ved godt mot, for Guds Engel, som jeg tjener, stod ved meg i natt og sa, 'Frykt ikke, Paulus.'” Er det riktig? Herrens Engel…
Hele Åpenbaringsboken ble skrevet etter vitnesbyrdet fra en Engel. "Jeg, Jesus, har sendt Min Engel for å vitne om disse tingene til menigheten." Er det riktig? 
Johannes falt ned for å tilbe Engelen. Engelen sa: "Gjør det ikke." Nå, det er ikke Joseph Smith. Men En sann Engel ville ikke stått for å bli tilbedt. Du vet det. Nei, Herren. Han sa, "Tilbe Gud."

text:
And God still has Angels, and they visit the people… Not a worship of Angels. But Angelic Beings who are attributes of God, sent forth to minister to the church, through the people. Oh, my. How short we are of the real apostolic church today of the blessing.
We people who claim to be there, who claim to have kissed the blessings of the rims of the cup of blessing, how short we come. How we bubble dance on top of the suds, and know not what the bottom is. That's true.
Now, friends, when I stand back here, I'm responsible, not to the audience, but to God. That's right. And my words must be… I know that I got many millions of people that listen and watch every word you say, so I must be, with all my heart, just as true to God as I can be. And I say today, that we've never scratched the surface. That's right. The church ought to get down. My. We… It's reading--It's like reading and writing, and arithmetic.
Notice. Now, this Angel came, He was Gabriel. Now, God sends minor Angels (That's right.), perhaps the One comes here. And some people will actually puff their mind up with Angels and things, who they think they've seen, and things like that. That's just the counterfeit.
text_no:
Gud har fortsatt Engler som besøker menneskene. Dette er ikke tilbedelse av Engler, men Engleve-sen som er egenskaper av Gud, sendt for å tjene Menigheten gjennom folket. Å, så kort vi er fra den virkelige apostoliske Menigheten i dag når det gjelder velsignelsen.
Vi som hevder å være der, som hevder å ha smakt velsignelsene fra kanten av velsignelsens beger, hvor lite vi egentlig kommer. Hvordan danser vi på toppen av skummet uten å forstå hva som er på bunnen. Det er sant.
Nå, venner, når jeg står her bak, er jeg ansvarlig, ikke overfor publikum, men overfor Gud. Det er riktig. Mine ord må være... Jeg vet at mange millioner mennesker lytter til og ser hvert ord jeg sier, så jeg må være, med hele mitt hjerte, så sann mot Gud som mulig. Og jeg sier i dag at vi aldri har skrapet overflaten. Det er riktig. Menigheten burde komme dypere. Å, vi... Det er som å lese, skrive og gjøre regning.
Merk dere dette. Denne Engelen som kom, var Gabriel. Gud sender også mindre Engler. Det er riktig. Kanskje Den Ene kommer hit. Noen mennesker vil faktisk oppblåse sitt sinn med Engler og ting de tror de har sett. Det er bare en etterligning.

text: If a man has seen an Angel of God, and met God has commissioned--that Angel commissioned him from God, and It was sent from God, it'll bear record right down to the line, that it's the truth. You believe that? But if it doesn't, it isn't the truth. That's one thing you can depend on, "By their fruits you shall know them." That's true.
text_no: Hvis en mann har sett en Guds Engel, og møtt Gud som har sendt denne Engelen, og Engelen er sendt fra Gud, vil det bekrefte sannheten nøyaktig. Tror du det? Men hvis det ikke skjer, er det ikke sant. Det er noe du kan stole på: "På fruktene skal dere kjenne dem." Det er sant.

text:
Now, notice this, that this Angel was Gabriel. Now, these minor Angels come; but when you see or hear of Gabriel coming, you get ready for something major fixing to happen. Gabriel was the one who announced the first coming of Jesus Christ. Is that right? And we're taught in the Bible that Gabriel will announce the second coming. He'll sound the trumpet of God (Is that right?), the coming of the Lord, the Angel of God, Gabriel, who stands at the, God's right hand.
Now, this great Gabriel come down in here, with the priest swinging his incense here, burning it, while the people was out praying. He was standing at the altar. And he looked over on the al--the side of the altar, and there stood the great Archangel. It frightened the priest. And He told him all about his life, and things, and what he was going to do. Said, "After days of your ministration here, go home to Elisabeth, your wife. And she's going to bear a child, and you'll call his name John."
Why, look. That priest now, said, "How can these things be? She's old." See?
text_no:
Legg merke til dette: Denne engelen var Gabriel. Nå kommer det vanligvis mindre engler; men når du ser eller hører om at Gabriel kommer, kan du forberede deg på at noe stort er i ferd med å skje. Gabriel var den som kunngjorde Jesu Kristi første komme. Er det riktig? Vi blir lært i Bibelen at Gabriel vil kunngjøre det andre komme. Han skal lyde Guds basun, (er det riktig?), Herrens komme, Guds Engel, Gabriel, som står ved Guds høyre hånd.
Nå kom denne store Gabriel ned her, mens presten svingte røkelsen og brant den, mens folket var ute og ba. Han sto ved alteret. Presten så over mot alteret, og der sto den store erkeengelen. Dette skremte presten. Gabriel fortalte ham alt om hans liv og hva han skulle gjøre. Han sa: "Etter dagene av din tjeneste her, gå hjem til Elisabeth, din kone. Hun skal føde et barn, og du skal kalle ham Johannes."
Se på dette. Presten spurte: "Hvordan kan dette skje? Hun er gammel." Ser du?

text:
There's righteous people walking in all the light of the Lord; that's the kind of a home that God can get into. Now, if you're serving all kinds of parties, and beer, and everything in your house, God will never visit you there. That's right. But if you've got a home that's cleaned up and living for God, with prayer and the Bible open, and a few tear stains on it, God can visit you. That's right. Because you've opened up a channel that He can come to. And there is where it was.
Now, when God had answered the prayer of Zacharias and Elisabeth, it liked to scared him to death, of course. He didn't think that it could happen. But He said, "I'm Gabriel, who stands in the presence of God, and My words shall be fulfilled in their season." And then he came back out, and the people… He was stricken dumb. And he was going to be dumb until the day the baby was born.
And then out come Zacharias, and the people… He beckoned to the people. They seen that he had seen a vision or something. So he went home, and Elisabeth conceived and hid herself six months. And she was to be a mother.
text_no:
Det finnes rettferdige mennesker som vandrer i alt Herrens lys; det er denne typen hjem Gud kan komme inn i. Hvis du derimot arrangerer alle slags fester, drikker øl og har alt annet i huset ditt, vil Gud aldri besøke deg der. Det er riktig. Men hvis du har et hjem som er renset og lever for Gud, med bønn, en åpen Bibel og noen tårer flekket på sidene, kan Gud besøke deg. Det stemmer, fordi du har åpnet en kanal som Han kan komme gjennom. Slik var det.
Da Gud svarte på bønnene til Sakarja og Elisabet, skremte det dem nesten til døde, naturligvis. De trodde ikke det kunne skje. Men Han sa: "Jeg er Gabriel, som står i Guds nærvær, og Mine ord skal oppfylles i deres tid." Da Sakarja kom tilbake ut, ble han stum. Han skulle forbli stum til dagen barnet ble født.
Så kom Sakarja ut, og folket… Han gjorde tegn til dem. De forstod at han hadde sett et syn eller noe lignende. Så dro han hjem, og Elisabet ble gravid og skjulte seg seks måneder. Hun skulle bli mor.

text:
Then watch, just down in Nazareth, the meanest city in the country, worse than Chicago, probably. Then, pretty near as bad as Jeffersonville, where I come from. Then the first thing you know, well, there, a little virgin one day, engaged to a man about forty-five years old, widower with about four children.
Why, she was coming home, in the oriental type. Probably it was on a Monday. She was packing her wash water on top of her head, maybe, in a jar as they do it, walking along. And all at once, before her flashed a big light. She… Frightened the little virgin. She looked. And there was Gabriel standing by her side. Said, "Hail Mary." Or "Stop." Said, "Blessed art thou among women." Said, told her how that her cousin Elisabeth had conceived in her old age, and was going to bear a child. And said the--that she was going to have a Child, knowing no man.
Now, look at the difference between Zacharias, that minister, who had studied the Scriptures over and over, and over and over, and over and over again, had plenty of examples: There was Sarah, and Hannah, and so forth, back in the Old Testament, plenty examples. He doubted it to be so. But this little girl didn't doubt. She said, "Behold the handmaid of the Lord; be it unto me according to Thy Word." What a difference.
text_no:
Nazareth, den mest foraktede byen i landet, verre enn Chicago, kanskje nesten like ille som Jeffersonville, hvor jeg kommer fra. I denne byen var det en dag en liten jomfru forlovet med en mann på rundt førtifem år, en enkemann med fire barn. 
En dag, kanskje en mandag, var hun på vei hjem i orientalsk stil, med vaskevann i en krukke på hodet. Mens hun gikk, blusset plutselig et stort lys opp foran henne. Det skremte den lille jomfruen, og hun så Gabriel stå ved siden av seg. Han sa: "Hill, Maria," eller "Stopp." Han fortsatte: "Velsignet er du blant kvinner," og fortalte hvordan hennes kusine Elisabeth hadde blitt gravid i sin alderdom og skulle føde et barn. Deretter sa han at Maria selv skulle få et Barn uten å ha kjent en mann.
Se på forskjellen mellom Sakarias, den presten som hadde studert Skriftene om og om igjen, og hadde mange eksempler som Sarah og Hanna fra Det Gamle Testamentet. Han tvilte på budskapet. Men den lille jenta tvilte ikke. Hun sa: "Se, Herrens tjenerinne; la det skje meg etter Ditt Ord." For en forskjell.

text:
Now, sometimes you can callous yourself. Reading the Bible, and just like reading a newspaper, and having about the same kind of faith that would, you have in one, then you callous yourself. When you read God's Word, believe It just like it says it is there, and take God at His Word.
Now, as soon as she read this, or she seen this before her, and the Angel of the Lord said, "You're going to have a baby." Look what she had to believe. Well, she had to believe something that had never happened. Zacharias didn't have to… Plenty times did that happen through the Scriptures. He had plenty of examples. But Mary had to believe something that was total impossible, and had never happened before. But yet, she didn't stagger at it; she just said, "Behold the handmaid of the Lord. Be it unto me." She took God at His Word.
And today, right here now, while hundreds of people around the world has been healed in these great campaigns in this latter revival that's sweeping the world. Right today, right at this minute, dozens, and scores of people, in meetings here in the United States, are being healed right now by the power of God; plenty of examples, every day, every night. And yet, we set and wonder, "God, why don't You do this," and, "why don't You do that?" Take God at His Word.
text_no:
Noen ganger kan man bli følelsesmessig avstumpet. Lesing av Bibelen kan bli likestilt med å lese en avis, med samme grad av tro som man har på avisen. Da blir man følelsesmessig avstumpet. Når du leser Guds Ord, tro på Det akkurat som Det står, og ta Gud på Hans Ord.
Så snart hun leste dette, eller så det foran seg, og Herrens Engel sa: "Du skal få en baby." Se hva hun måtte tro. Hun måtte tro på noe som aldri hadde skjedd før. Sakarias trengte ikke... Det hadde skjedd mange ganger gjennom Skriftene. Han hadde mange eksempler. Men Maria måtte tro på noe som var helt umulig og aldri hadde skjedd før. Likevel vaklet hun ikke; hun sa bare: "Se, jeg er Herrens tjenestekvinne. La det skje med meg." Hun tok Gud på Hans Ord.
I dag, akkurat nå, mens hundrevis av mennesker rundt om i verden har blitt helbredet i disse store kampanjene i denne siste vekkelsen som feier over verden. Akkurat i dag, akkurat i dette øyeblikk, blir dusinvis og titalls mennesker helbredet her i møtene i USA av Guds kraft; mange eksempler, hver dag, hver natt. Og likevel sitter vi og undrer oss: "Gud, hvorfor gjør Du ikke dette," og, "hvorfor gjør Du ikke det?" Ta Gud på Hans Ord.

text:
Mary said, "Behold the handmaid of the Lord." She started rejoicing. She was happy. God had promised it; she knowed she was going to have the baby. I believe I spoke something about it last night. She didn't wait till she felt life. She didn't wait till she was positive. She didn't wait till any visible sign. She just believed what the Angel said was the truth. There you are.
The trouble of it is, we question God. And if we question God, we can't believe God. Faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things you don't see, taste, feel, smell, or hear. Amen.
I begin to feel a little religious right now. I… All right. Oh, take God at His Word. She begin to rejoice and praise God, just as soon as she heard. "Faith cometh by hearing, hearing by the Word of God." Is that right? "Faith cometh by hearing."
text_no:
Mary sa: "Se, Herrens tjenerinne." Hun begynte å juble og var glad. Gud hadde lovet det, og hun visste at hun skulle få barnet. Jeg tror jeg sa noe om det i går kveld. Hun ventet ikke til hun kjente liv. Hun ventet ikke til hun var sikker. Hun ventet ikke på noen synlige tegn. Hun bare trodde at det engelen sa, var sannheten. Der har du det.
Problemet er at vi stiller spørsmål ved Gud. Og hvis vi stiller spørsmål ved Gud, kan vi ikke tro på Ham. Tro er en overbevisning om det vi håper, og en visshet om ting vi ikke ser, smaker, føler, lukter eller hører. Amen.
Jeg begynner å føle meg litt religiøs akkurat nå. Alt i orden. Å ta Gud på Hans Ord. Hun begynte å juble og prise Gud så snart hun hørte det. "Troen kommer av forkynnelsen, og forkynnelsen ved Kristi Ord." Er det riktig? "Troen kommer av forkynnelsen."

text:
Soon as you hear the Word of God that says you can be set free, believe it. Rejoice with it. God will show the evidence after you've accepted it. But first you accept it.
Christ died for sinners. I believe it. I accept it. I start rejoicing. God works righteousness by it.
I believe He was striped for my healing. I believe it. I accept it. God's works healing for me, see. Just as soon as I believe. He can't help me till I believe. I've got to believe and confess it first, and then God's under obligation to His Word.
And if He's Almighty God, He can do all things. And if He cannot do all things, He's not Almighty God. There it is. All right.
Notice. What kind of a boy ought this to be? What kind of a person? God keeping His Word, God's promised to do anything, God will do it.
When the children of Israel was led out of Egypt, God promised to take care of them as they journeyed through the wilderness. God said He would take care of them, and He did do it. No matter where the path led, any enemy got in God's path, it give away.
I can see them come up to the Red Sea. God had His map drawed out, way He was going to take them. They will move right up to the Red Sea: no way at all across it. Here was Pharaoh's army, the mountains on one side, Pharaoh's army here, the Red Sea in front of them. Moses lifted up his hands. Psalms 72 said God looked down. He seen that path laying through the bottom of the sea. There was His children right there, needing the promise, taking God at His Word.
text_no:
Så snart du hører Guds Ord som sier at du kan bli satt fri, tro det. Gled deg over det. Gud vil vise beviset etter at du har akseptert det. Men først må du akseptere det.
Kristus døde for syndere. Jeg tror det. Jeg aksepterer det. Jeg begynner å glede meg. Gud virker rettferdighet ved det.
